final-neighborhood-resource-guide-histor.label: Final Capstone: Neighborhood Guide History
final-neighborhood-resource-guide-histor.summary: Build a compact four-file baseline and a clean four-commit local history.

try_final_neighborhood_resource_guide_hi.title: Clean Tracked Paths and Recover a Mistake
Commit and Audit the Final Handoff
Initialize and Record the Guide Baseline
try_final_neighborhood_resource_guide_hi.prompt: Inside `neighborhood-guide/`, initialize a repository on `main` and store the supplied local author name and email. Stage the four baseline text files—`README.md`, `services.txt`, `hours.txt`, and `old-contacts.txt`—then record them with the message `Start neighborhood guide`.
try_final_neighborhood_resource_guide_hi.hint: Build the first snapshot from the four supplied text files.

help.concept: This capstone combines the Module 1 workflow: initialize, configure local identity, stage the named files, and commit the first snapshot. No programming knowledge is involved.
help.hint_1: Use the exact four paths and supplied identity.
help.hint_2: Do not create additional files in this step.

try_final_neighborhood_resource_guide_hi.starterCode:
# Neighborhood Guide

Local services and opening hours.
try_final_neighborhood_resource_guide_hi.solutionCode:
git init -b main
git config --local user.name "ZoeSkoul Learner"
git config --local user.email "learner@zoeskoul.local"
git add README.md services.txt hours.txt old-contacts.txt
git commit -m "Start neighborhood guide"
try_final_neighborhood_resource_guide_hi.instructions: Inside `neighborhood-guide/`, initialize a repository on `main` and store the supplied local author name and email. Stage the four baseline text files—`README.md`, `services.txt`, `hours.txt`, and `old-contacts.txt`—then record them with the message `Start neighborhood guide`.

zoeskoul_setup_sh.content:
#!/usr/bin/env bash
set -eu
rm -rf -- neighborhood-guide
mkdir -p -- neighborhood-guide
mkdir -p -- neighborhood-guide
printf %s '# Neighborhood Guide

Local services and opening hours.
' > neighborhood-guide/README.md
mkdir -p -- neighborhood-guide
printf %s 'Help desk hours: 10 AM - 4 PM
' > neighborhood-guide/hours.txt
mkdir -p -- neighborhood-guide
printf %s 'Old contact list

Retired phone tree.
' > neighborhood-guide/old-contacts.txt
mkdir -p -- neighborhood-guide
printf %s 'Neighborhood services

Welcome desk
Food pantry
' > neighborhood-guide/services.txt

try_final_neighborhood_resource_guide_hi.title: Update the Neighborhood Services
try_final_neighborhood_resource_guide_hi.prompt: Open `neighborhood-guide/services.txt`. Add `Health clinic` on a new line immediately after `Food pantry`. Save the file. Inspect the unstaged diff, stage the file, and commit it with the message `Update neighborhood services`.
try_final_neighborhood_resource_guide_hi.hint: Make the exact one-line addition and record it as one focused purpose.

help.concept: `git diff -- services.txt` reviews the added service before staging. Staging and committing only that path creates a focused second snapshot without adding another project file.
help.hint_1: Do not change the existing service lines.

try_final_neighborhood_resource_guide_hi.starterCode:
# Neighborhood Guide

Local services and opening hours.
try_final_neighborhood_resource_guide_hi.solutionCode:
git diff -- services.txt
git add services.txt
git commit -m "Update neighborhood services"
try_final_neighborhood_resource_guide_hi.instructions: Open `neighborhood-guide/services.txt`. Add `Health clinic` on a new line immediately after `Food pantry`. Save the file. Inspect the unstaged diff, stage the file, and commit it with the message `Update neighborhood services`.

zoeskoul_setup_sh.content:
#!/usr/bin/env bash
set -eu
rm -rf -- neighborhood-guide
mkdir -p -- neighborhood-guide
git -C neighborhood-guide init -q -b main
git -C neighborhood-guide config --local user.name 'ZoeSkoul Learner'
git -C neighborhood-guide config --local user.email learner@zoeskoul.local
find neighborhood-guide -mindepth 1 -maxdepth 1 ! -name .git -exec rm -rf -- {} +
mkdir -p -- neighborhood-guide
printf %s '# Neighborhood Guide

Local services and opening hours.
' > neighborhood-guide/README.md
mkdir -p -- neighborhood-guide
printf %s 'Help desk hours: 10 AM - 4 PM
' > neighborhood-guide/hours.txt
mkdir -p -- neighborhood-guide
printf %s 'Old contact list

Retired phone tree.
' > neighborhood-guide/old-contacts.txt
mkdir -p -- neighborhood-guide
printf %s 'Neighborhood services

Welcome desk
Food pantry
' > neighborhood-guide/services.txt
git -C neighborhood-guide add -A
GIT_AUTHOR_DATE=2026-01-01T12:00:00Z GIT_COMMITTER_DATE=2026-01-01T12:00:00Z git -C neighborhood-guide commit -q -m 'Start neighborhood guide'

printf %s 'Neighborhood services

' > neighborhood-guide/services.txt

try_final_neighborhood_resource_guide_hi.prompt: Open `neighborhood-guide/hours.txt`. Replace `Help desk hours: 10 AM - 4 PM` with `Help desk hours: 9 AM - 5 PM`. Save the file. Inspect the unstaged diff, stage it, and commit it with the message `Update help desk hours`.
try_final_neighborhood_resource_guide_hi.hint: Review the exact hours change before recording it.

help.concept: The same focused workflow applies: diff the single file, stage it by name, and commit it as its own purpose.
help.hint_2: The third commit should affect only `hours.txt`.

try_final_neighborhood_resource_guide_hi.title: Clean Paths and Recover a README Mistake
try_final_neighborhood_resource_guide_hi.prompt: Open `neighborhood-guide/README.md`. Replace `Local services and opening hours.` with `Accidental rewrite.` and save. Stage that mistake, rename tracked `services.txt` to `community-services.txt`, and remove tracked `old-contacts.txt`. Then unstage and restore only the README mistake. Inspect the staged name-status summary; it should contain the rename and removal, but not `README.md`.
try_final_neighborhood_resource_guide_hi.hint: Keep the intended path maintenance staged while recovering the accidental README edit.

help.concept: Use `git mv` and `git rm` for the intended changes. Use `git restore --staged README.md` followed by `git restore README.md` to remove the mistake from both current layers without disturbing the other staged work.
help.hint_2: The final staged summary should contain only the rename and removal.

try_final_neighborhood_resource_guide_hi.starterCode:
# Neighborhood Guide

Local services and opening hours.
try_final_neighborhood_resource_guide_hi.solutionCode:
git add README.md
git mv services.txt community-services.txt
git rm old-contacts.txt
git restore --staged README.md
git restore README.md
git diff --staged --name-status
try_final_neighborhood_resource_guide_hi.instructions: Open `neighborhood-guide/README.md`. Replace `Local services and opening hours.` with `Accidental rewrite.` and save. Stage that mistake, rename tracked `services.txt` to `community-services.txt`, and remove tracked `old-contacts.txt`. Then unstage and restore only the README mistake. Inspect the staged name-status summary; it should contain the rename and removal, but not `README.md`.

try_final_neighborhood_resource_guide_hi.title: Add the Generated-File Policy
try_final_neighborhood_resource_guide_hi.prompt: Create `neighborhood-guide/.gitignore` with exactly `generated/` on line 1. Create `neighborhood-guide/generated/preview.txt` containing `Temporary neighborhood preview.` Inspect status with ignored paths included, then stage only `.gitignore`. Keep the rename and removal staged.
try_final_neighborhood_resource_guide_hi.hint: Add one policy file and one ignored preview after the obsolete file has already been removed.

help.concept: The `generated/` rule keeps the preview outside future snapshots. `git status --short --ignored` verifies it with `!!`, while `git add .gitignore` adds only the policy to the existing maintenance proposal.
help.hint_2: The staged set should contain the rename, removal, and `.gitignore`.

try_final_neighborhood_resource_guide_hi.title: Commit and Audit the Final Handoff
try_final_neighborhood_resource_guide_hi.prompt: Record the prepared maintenance snapshot with the message `Prepare neighborhood guide handoff`. Then inspect concise history, the newest commit summary, and final short status. The capstone should end with four focused commits and no unfinished tracked work.
try_final_neighborhood_resource_guide_hi.hint: Commit once, then verify timeline, latest snapshot, and current state.

help.concept: The final commit records the ignore policy, service-file rename, and obsolete-file removal. The three inspection commands confirm the complete four-commit story and clean handoff.
help.hint_1: The ignored preview remains on disk but outside normal status.
help.hint_2: The newest commit should be the maintenance handoff.

try_final_neighborhood_resource_guide_hi.starterCode:

try_final_neighborhood_resource_guide_hi.solutionCode:
git commit -m "Prepare neighborhood guide handoff"
git log --oneline
git show --stat --oneline HEAD
git status --short
try_final_neighborhood_resource_guide_hi.instructions: Record the prepared maintenance snapshot with the message `Prepare neighborhood guide handoff`. Then inspect concise history, the newest commit summary, and final short status. The capstone should end with four focused commits and no unfinished tracked work.

finalCapstone.description: The capstone starts in `neighborhood-guide/` with no history from the other projects. File edits are still supplied exactly because this is a Git assessment. Your responsibility is to apply initialization, focused history, path maintenance, ignore rules, safe restore, and final verification across six cumulative steps.
finalCapstone.title: Complete the Neighborhood Guide Capstone

intro.title: Fresh Repository, Independent Transfer
intro.bodyMarkdown: The capstone starts in `neighborhood-guide/` with four small text files. It never assumes programming knowledge. Across six cumulative steps, use the Git workflow independently: initialize, create focused commits, add one ignore policy, maintain tracked paths, recover a mistake, and verify a clean four-commit handoff.
